location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: Individual-contributor leadership role: you set the strategy and the bar
Move the organization through influence, evidence, and craft rather than headcount
Base pay range $176,000 - $220,000 consistent with Staff PM at a major tech company
Represent Business Travel in executive reviews and cross-org forums

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of product management experience or equivalent, with a meaningful portion owning growth, acquisition, lifecycle, or funnel work at scale on a consumer mobile product.
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful portion in growth/acquisition/lifecycle/funnel on consumer mobile product

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Track record of measurable, durable impact on acquisition, activation, or retention metrics that you can defend.
Demonstrated ownership of broad, ambiguous problem areas that span teams. You have set a multi-year growth strategy and built the loops or infrastructure that compounded across products.
Comfort serving two customers at once, the individual user and the organization that pays, with a feel for where consumer growth tactics and B2B or B2B2C motions meet.
Quantitative rigor and experimentation discipline. You think in funnels, cohorts, and growth loops. You size opportunities, design clean A/B tests, and read results without overclaiming.
Fluency with the modern growth stack: lifecycle and CRM platforms, attribution, product analytics (Amplitude, Mixpanel, or similar), and experimentation tooling.
Experience building or scaling acquisition through partnerships and third-party integrations, with a feel for the integration, incentive, and measurement work that makes a partner channel durable.

Exceptional communication and influence at every altitude, from an engineer debating instrumentation to a VP sponsoring the target. You operate independently in a flat, fast-moving organization and move teams to act without relying on authority.

scope.surface: Business Travel full-funnel growth — individual business travelers and managed company accounts, spanning acquisition, onboarding, activation, engagement, and retention
scope.business_model: B2B2C — individual consumer rideshare with enterprise/managed company accounts layer
primary_metrics_inferred: New business rider acquisition (self-serve, paid, organic, referral, partner channels)
core_responsibilities: Define the full-funnel growth strategy for business travel across acquisition, activation, and engagement, spanning both organic business travelers and managed company accounts
Own new-user acquisition for business travel across self-serve enrollment, paid, organic, referral, and partner channels
Drive onboarding, activation, and first value from signup through first business ride
Build partnership- and integration-powered channels with travel booking, expense platforms, corporate cards, loyalty programs, airlines, hotels, and HR/finance systems
Design engagement and retention loops including business rewards, lifecycle messaging, in-app re-engagement
Set the experimentation bar including testing cadence, readout standards, and shipping bar
Translate funnel data, cohort behavior, and experiment results into quarterly priorities and represent Business Travel in executive reviews

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($176K-$220K base) with equity and bonus mentioned
Clear product surface and ownership area with well-defined funnel stages
Individual-contributor role with explicit influence-based leadership model (no direct reports required)
Business travel framed as 'one of Lyft's largest and most durable growth opportunities' — high strategic visibility
Hybrid flexibility of up to 4 weeks remote per year in addition to 3-day in-office schedule
Detailed responsibilities show strategic depth expected, not just execution
red_flags: Hybrid requires 3 specific days per week (Mon/Wed/Thu) in office — limited flexibility on schedule even if technically hybrid
New York City area only for the stated pay range; other locations not mentioned, limiting candidate geography
Visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD
No nice-to-haves listed — hard requirement bar is high with no partial credit signals

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years requirement, compensation range, and IC leadership framing)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not enumerated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description of individual travelers + managed company accounts)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'salary ranges are dependent on… geographic location')
ambiguities: The JD names New York City for the compensation range but does not explicitly state the role is NYC-only; other locations may exist but are not disclosed. Treated as NYC-primary based on stated pay range.
Nice-to-haves section is entirely absent from the JD — all listed requirements appear as unconditional bullets, so all were classified as must_haves with severity hard.
